rationale.root_cause: They identified the memcpy at the flaw site (program.c ~line 45) using sizeof(structCharVoid) to copy into charFirst, which overwrites adjacent struct members (voidSecond) causing a stack buffer overflow. They explained the concrete mechanism of overwriting the pointer and the resulting crash on printLine.
rationale.local_fix: They explicitly instructed to change sizeof(structCharVoid) to sizeof(structCharVoid.charFirst) in the memcpy call. This directly prevents overwriting the pointer field.
rationale.global_fix: They suggested policies/tools and hardening flags but did not propose representing the destination as a bounded buffer/span tying copy length to the field capacity. Thus, the required global fix was not provided.
